Bindings for the Arduino Servo library (Servo.h).

Reference: https://github.com/arduino-libraries/Servo This library allows an Arduino board to control RC (hobby) servo motors. Wraps the Servo class which drives hobby servos via timer-based PWM.

Types

Servo {.importcpp: "Servo".} = object

Class for manipulating servo motors connected to Arduino pins. Servos are pulsed in the background using the value most recently written using the write() method.

Note that analogWrite of PWM on pins associated with the timer are disabled when the first servo is attached. Timers are seized as needed in groups of 12 servos — 24 servos use two timers, 48 servos will use four.

Consts

DEFAULT_PULSE_WIDTH = 1500'i32
Default pulse width when servo is attached (microseconds) 
INVALID_SERVO = 255'u
Flag indicating an invalid servo index 
MAX_PULSE_WIDTH = 2400'i32
The longest pulse sent to a servo (microseconds) 
MIN_PULSE_WIDTH = 544'i32
The shortest pulse sent to a servo (microseconds) 
REFRESH_INTERVAL = 20000'i32
Minimum time to refresh servos in microseconds

Procs

proc attach(s: var Servo; pin: cint): uint8 {.importcpp, discardable,
    header: "Servo.h", ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Attaches a servo motor to an I/O pin. Returns channel number or INVALID_SERVO on failure. 
proc attach(s: var Servo; pin: cint; min: cint; max: cint): uint8 {.importcpp,
    discardable, header: "Servo.h", ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Attaches to a pin setting min and max pulse widths in microseconds. Default min is 544, max is 2400. 
proc attached(s: Servo): bool {.importcpp, header: "Servo.h", ...raises: [],
                                tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns true if there is a servo attached. 
proc detach(s: var Servo) {.importcpp, header: "Servo.h", ...raises: [], tags: [],
                            forbids: [].}
Stops an attached servo from pulsing its I/O pin. 
proc initServo(): Servo {.importcpp: "Servo()", constructor, header: "Servo.h",
                          ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc read(s: Servo): cint {.importcpp, header: "Servo.h", ...raises: [], tags: [],
                            forbids: [].}
Gets the last written servo pulse width as an angle between 0 and 180. 
proc readMicroseconds(s: Servo): cint {.importcpp, header: "Servo.h",
                                        ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Gets the last written servo pulse width in microseconds. 
proc write(s: var Servo; value: cint) {.importcpp, header: "Servo.h",
                                        ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Sets the servo angle in degrees (0 to 180). If value is treated as invalid angle but valid as pulse width in microseconds, it is treated as microseconds. 
proc writeMicroseconds(s: var Servo; value: cint) {.importcpp,
    header: "Servo.h", ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Sets the servo pulse width in microseconds.
